signature.instructions:
Task
Categorize and lightly clean organization/person/legal-entity names into four fields: name, alias, weakAlias, and previousName.

Inputs
- entity_schema: One of LegalEntity, Organization, Person. If it is unrecognized (e.g., Company), treat it as LegalEntity/Organization.
- strings: A list of raw strings to process.

Outputs
Return a JSON-like object with four array fields. Always include all four arrays; leave empty when no values apply.
- name: []
- alias: []
- weakAlias: []
- previousName: []
Rules for arrays
- Case-insensitive de-duplication within each field; keep the first-occurrence casing.
- Do not place the same exact cleaned string into more than one field.
- Preserve the order encountered after processing.
- Discard any string that becomes empty after cleaning.

General cleaning (minimal, non-destructive)
1) Do not invent, expand, shorten, or derive names that are not explicitly present in the input.
   - Do not output “TMR” if the input is “TMR LTD”.
   - Do not remove legal suffixes.
2) Trim leading/trailing whitespace.
3) Collapse internal multiple spaces to a single space.
4) Remove only obvious non-name wrapping characters:
   - Surrounding quotes (single/double), and leading/trailing brackets or parentheses when they wrap the entire string.
   - Recognize standard and non-ASCII/fullwidth forms of quotes/brackets/parentheses, e.g., （）, 〔〕, 【】, ｢｣.
   - Leading/trailing punctuation such as stray commas, semicolons, or pipes.
   - Preserve internal punctuation that commonly appears in names: ampersand (&), apostrophe ('), hyphen (-), slash (/), and periods used in abbreviations.
5) Normalize dotted abbreviations by removing dots from common legal/organizational abbreviations (e.g., “L.T.D.” → “LTD”, “S.A.” → “SA”). Do not change letter casing.
6) Decode common HTML entities (including numeric forms):
   - & → &
   - ' or ' → '
   - " → "
   - < → <
   - > → >
   Keep any resulting internal punctuation (including apostrophes) unless it is clearly just a surrounding wrapper.
7) A space is never a delimiter.

Marker-based splitting (alias/previousName)
- Only split a single input string into multiple outputs when there is a clear delimiter or explicit marker:
  • Delimiters: “;” or “|” explicitly separate distinct names.
  • Markers (case-insensitive): “aka”, “a/k/a”, “also known as”, “d/b/a”, “doing business as”, “trading as”, “t/a”, “operates as”, “formerly”, “formerly known as”, “f/k/a”, “p/k/a”, “previously known as”, “previously called”, “previously named”, “ex-”, “anciennement”.
- Markers may appear anywhere, including within parentheses or after punctuation. Extract only the indicated alternate/previous name(s) and remove the marker words themselves from the captured value.
- Parenthetical segments that contain markers (e.g., “(FORMERLY …)”) should be parsed to extract the marked names; do not discard them as mere wrappers.
- If multiple alternate/previous names are provided after a marker, split them using explicit delimiters (“;” or “|”) and add each to the correct field.
- Everything before the first alias/previous marker is treated as the base candidate for name/weakAlias (after cleaning), if non-empty.

Parenthetical variant aliases without explicit markers (important edge case)
- If a base name is followed by a parenthetical segment that lists one or more variant spellings (commonly transliterations) without explicit markers, treat those variants as aliases. This is common for personal names.
  • Recognize parentheses in ASCII and non-ASCII/fullwidth forms: (), （）, 〔〕, 【】.
  • Within such a parenthetical variant list, allow “/” as a delimiter between variants. Do not treat “/” as a general delimiter elsewhere.
  • Construct each alias by combining the base given name(s) with each listed variant when the parenthetical content appears to be a surname or component replacement. Example:
    - Input: “Elmira Khaimourzina（Khaimurzina/Haimurzyna）”
    - Base name: “Elmira Khaimourzina”
    - Aliases: “Elmira Khaimurzina”, “Elmira Haimurzyna”
  • If the parenthetical variants look like complete alternate full names, use them as-is for alias.
  • Do not place these variants in previousName unless explicit “former/previous” markers are present.

Schema-specific categorization
If entity_schema is unrecognized (e.g., “Company”), apply the LegalEntity/Organization rules.

Person
- name:
  - Use for the cleaned person name as given when it appears to be a full personal name (e.g., given name + surname), preserving internal slashes, hyphens, particles, patronymics, etc. (e.g., “Manoharan A/L Malayalam”).
- alias and previousName:
  - Populate only when explicit markers indicate alternate or former personal names, or when parsing parenthetical variant lists (see “Parenthetical variant aliases…” above).
- weakAlias:
  - Use when the entire cleaned string is:
    • an acronym/initialism, or
    • a mononym, nisba/epithet, kunya, or surname-only style label rather than a clear full personal name (e.g., single word or hyphenated forms like “Al-Kimawi”, “al-Masri”, “Abu Khalid”).
  - Otherwise, prefer name for clear full personal names.

LegalEntity and Organization
- name (default for well-formed organization/legal-entity names):
  - Use when the cleaned string appears to be a full, well-formed organization/legal-entity name.
  - Retain standard legal suffixes as part of the name (e.g., Ltd, Limited, Inc, LLC, PLC, GmbH, Sarl, BV, NV, OOO, LLP, Co., Corp, SA, SAS, BVBA, AG, KK, Pty Ltd, Pte. Ltd., JSC, etc.).
  - IMPORTANT: Do not automatically classify an all-uppercase string as weakAlias. Many sources write names in all caps. Prefer name for all-caps strings unless they match the weakAlias patterns below.
- weakAlias (use when uncertain, stylized, or acronymic):
  - Use for acronyms/initialisms or short uppercase strings (typical signals: length ≤ 6, vowel-poor, clearly initialisms, e.g., “UWSA”).
  - Use for all-uppercase “compact/registration-style” strings that include a legal suffix and read like a condensed registration line (e.g., “TMR LTD”, “VTB 24 JSC”, “ACME INC”, “GLOBAL CO LTD”). Keep the full string intact, including the suffix.
  - Use for obviously stylized all-caps compressed brand forms (e.g., “TRUCLEAN”).
  - Otherwise (e.g., multi-word all-caps company names or longer coined words), prefer name.
- alias:
  - Populate only when explicit markers indicate an alternate/trading name, or when parenthetical variant lists clearly provide alternates (primarily for person-like strings; see below).
- previousName:
  - Populate only when explicit markers indicate a former/previous name.
- Person-like strings under LegalEntity/Organization:
  - If a string clearly looks like a personal name (e.g., given name + surname) and lacks organizational cues/suffixes, treat it like a Person for parsing and classification:
    • Put the full base personal name in name (not weakAlias), unless it matches the Person weakAlias patterns.
    • Apply the parenthetical variant alias rule to extract aliases from trailing parentheses with slash-delimited variants.

Additional guidance and edge cases
- HTML entity decoding matters. Example: “YANTAI EVER GROWING INT&#39'” should be cleaned to include the decoded apostrophe character rather than the raw “&#39” entity. Do not drop the apostrophe produced by decoding unless it is clearly just a surrounding wrapper.
- When in doubt between name and weakAlias for an all-caps organization string, prefer name unless it is clearly:
  • an acronym/initialism (e.g., “UWSA”), or
  • a compact registration-style line with a legal suffix (e.g., “TMR LTD”, “VTB 24 JSC”).
- Hyphenated or longer coined all-caps words without legal suffixes (e.g., “YAMAKEN-GUMI”, “TRIANGULATICA”) are typically name.
- Never split on “/” unless it occurs inside parenthetical variant lists as described above. Preserve “/” that are part of the name itself (e.g., “A/L” in Malaysian names).

Implementation tips
1) For each input string:
   - Clean (trim, collapse spaces, decode HTML entities, normalize dotted abbreviations, remove only leading/trailing wrappers and stray punctuation).
   - Detect and extract alias/previous segments anywhere in the string (including within parentheses); split by explicit delimiters (“;” or “|”) only.
   - Additionally, detect parenthetical variant lists without markers (ASCII or fullwidth parentheses). When present:
     • Treat their contents as alias candidates; if variants are partial components (e.g., surnames), combine with the base given name(s) to form full aliases.
     • Within these parentheses, split variants by “/”.
   - The remaining base segment (if any) is the candidate for name/weakAlias classification under the schema rules (with Person-like override as above).
2) Classification:
   - Person: base → name if full personal name; otherwise → weakAlias if mononym/epithet/nisba/kunya.
   - LegalEntity/Organization (incl. “Company”):
     • If the string is clearly a personal name, apply Person logic (including parenthetical variant aliases).
     • Else, if short all-caps initialism or compact registration-style with suffix → weakAlias.
     • Else → name.
3) Add values to their respective arrays, deduplicating case-insensitively and never duplicating across fields.

Validation against known feedback (must match)
- UWSA → weakAlias: ["UWSA"]
- TMR LTD → weakAlias: ["TMR LTD"] (do not add “TMR” anywhere)
- TRUCLEAN → weakAlias: ["TRUCLEAN"]
- TRIANGULATICA (LegalEntity) → name: ["TRIANGULATICA"]
- YANTAI EVER GROWING INT&#39' (Organization) → name should contain the decoded apostrophe form (not the raw “&#39” entity).
- Manoharan A/L Malayalam (Person) → name: ["Manoharan A/L Malayalam"]
- BANK OF CHONGQING CO., LTD. ( FORMERLY CHONGQING COMMERCIAL BANK) (Company) →
  • name: ["BANK OF CHONGQING CO., LTD."]
  • previousName: ["CHONGQING COMMERCIAL BANK"]
- VTB 24 JSC (LegalEntity) → weakAlias: ["VTB 24 JSC"]
- YAMAKEN-GUMI (LegalEntity) → name: ["YAMAKEN-GUMI"]
- PATRICIA O'KANE (Person) → name: ["PATRICIA O'KANE"]
- Elmira Khaimourzina（Khaimurzina/Haimurzyna） (LegalEntity) →
  • name: ["Elmira Khaimourzina"]
  • alias: ["Elmira Khaimurzina", "Elmira Haimurzyna"]
- Al-Kimawi (Person) → weakAlias: ["Al-Kimawi"]

fields.description: The schema denotes the type of entity. Both Persons and Organizations are specialisation of LegalEntity. Company is a specialisation of Organization. Everything extends Thing.
A list of raw name strings to be cleaned and categorised. Each string might contain multiple names.
A list of the primary names of this entity, potentially in various languages and transliterations.
A list of alternative but still fully descriptive names for this entity.
A list of names with low confidence or a very low degree of uniqueness in the context of legal entity names. Includes clear nicknames with no similarity to the full name.
A list of names this entity was known by in the past.
